violated_invariant: Before liquidation/account-health accounting, cumulativeInterestRate[token] must reflect elapsed blocks since lastBlockAccrued, and borrower debt/account snapshots must be accrued against that current market index.
evidence_source: TOSEM Dynamic Dependent Update Omission row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 18 H-02; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/18/contracts/LendingPair.sol:179-190,246-289,386-394,456-460.
desynchronization_step: liquidateAccount() calls _accrueAccountInterest() and accountHealth() without first calling accrue(), so the global interest index is not advanced for elapsed blocks.
strict_match_notes: B0 row 18:2 reports lastBlockAccrued/accrue() and includes H-02 as adjacent evidence, but the oracle bug is the liquidateAccount() omission and liquidation/accounting sink.

source_root_cause: Token balances are not promptly updated when the contract switches revenue sources.
source_exploitation_method: The calculation of balance and shares is incorrect, resulting in users receiving undeserved shares.
violated_invariant: The active yieldSource pointer, the yield source balance for this contract, and swappable-share supply must describe the same underlying asset pool before shares are minted or balances are quoted.
evidence_source: TOSEM Dynamic Dependent Update Omission row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 24 H-03; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/24/in_scope/SwappableYieldSource.sol:148-176,208-226,254-310.
coupled_state_entities: yieldSource, old yieldSource.balanceOfToken(address(this)), new yieldSource.balanceOfToken(address(this)), ERC20 totalSupply(), user share balances
primary_state_entity: underlying assets held in the old/current yield source
counterpart_state_entity: yieldSource pointer and swappable share accounting
desynchronization_step: setYieldSource() switches yieldSource before transferFunds() moves old underlying balances; supplyTokenTo() then mints shares using only the new yield source balance.
strict_match_notes: Evaluated repo sheets exist but no same finding tag appears in MV-Bench.

source_root_cause: The auctionBurn function does not update ibRatio when burning auction bonds.
source_exploitation_method: The calculation of total supply was incorrect, resulting in some assets being frozen.

source_root_cause: The total value locked (TVL) is not promptly updated when users deposit or withdraw.
source_exploitation_method: Wrong token share calculated using outdated TVL.
violated_invariant: Vault share mint/burn calculations must use a TVL cache synchronized with the actual rebasing aToken balances before deposit or withdrawal changes share supply.
evidence_source: TOSEM Dynamic Dependent Update Omission row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 58 H-04; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/58/mellow-vaults/contracts/AaveVault.sol:25-94 and dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/58/mellow-vaults/contracts/LpIssuer.sol:84-128.
coupled_state_entities: AaveVault._tvls cached TVL, aToken.balanceOf(vault), LP token totalSupply/user balances
counterpart_state_entity: cached AaveVault._tvls used for share pricing
desynchronization_step: deposit/withdraw computes mint or burn share amounts from old cached TVL, then transferAndPush/TVL update observes rebased interest.
strict_match_notes: Evaluated repo sheet exists but no same finding tag appears in MV-Bench.

source_exploitation_method: The attacker uses the lagged previousPrices to calculate the liquidity assessment and manipulate the liquidity share.

source_root_cause: The actionLockedBalances[payer] is not updated for the payer when transferring stakes.
mvbench_all_matched_rows: sheet=112backd | row=199 | abl=B3 | type=TP | finding=[multi_var_cross_contract] {actionLockedBalances, balances} tx-set -> increaseActionLockedBalance(address,uint256), transferFrom(address,address,uint256) • write contracts/StakerVault.sol:169 (StakerVault.('transferFrom', ['address', 'address', 'uint256'], ['bool'])) • read contracts/StakerVault.sol:209 (StakerVault.('increaseActionLockedBalance', ['address', 'uint256'], ['bool'])) | report= | comments=[H-02] Direct match from our original build motivation -- this is a confirmed multi-var invariant matching ground truth from during our initial build of MV-SCAN
violated_invariant: A payer’s stakedAndActionLockedBalanceOf must equal ordinary staked balance plus stakes transferred into active action locks, so deposit caps, fees, and reward checkpoints see locked stake.
evidence_source: TOSEM Dynamic Dependent Update Omission row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 112 H-02; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/112/backd/contracts/actions/topup/TopUpAction.sol:36-67 and dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/112/backd/contracts/StakerVault.sol:133-210.
coupled_state_entities: StakerVault.balanceOf(payer), StakerVault.actionLockedBalances[payer], TopUp position depositTokenBalance, stakedAndActionLockedBalanceOf(payer)
primary_state_entity: stakes transferred from payer into TopUp action lock
desynchronization_step: TopUpActionLibrary.lockFunds() transfers stakes from payer but omits stakerVault.increaseActionLockedBalance(payer, amountLeft).
strict_match_notes: Same finding tag appears only outside B0 in MV-Bench.

source_root_cause: The Lock.extendLock function increases the locked amount but does not update the total locked amount.
source_exploitation_method: An accounting error resulted in the inability to release locked assets added through the extendLock() function in the future.
violated_invariant: For each asset, Lock.totalLocked[asset] must equal the sum of unreleased BondNFT lock amounts, including principal added through extendLock().
evidence_source: TOSEM Dynamic Dependent Update Omission row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 192 H-01; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/192/contracts/Lock.sol:19-24,61-105.
coupled_state_entities: Lock.totalLocked[asset], BondNFT lock amount, ERC20 asset balance locked in Lock
desynchronization_step: extendLock() transfers/increases the bond amount through BondNFT.extendLock() but does not increment totalLocked[asset].
strict_match_notes: Evaluated repo sheet exists but no same finding tag appears in MV-Bench.

source_root_cause: The granted NFT transaction authorization is not revoked.
source_exploitation_method: The authorization has not been revoked, and the authorized person can transfer NFTs repeatedly.
violated_invariant: A delegate approval for a vault-held ERC721 must be revocable and must not remain valid after the authorized transfer changes possession/ownership context.
evidence_source: TOSEM permission-management subtype row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 10 H-02; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/10/contracts/contracts/visor/Visor.sol:481-516.
coupled_state_entities: nftApprovals/delegate approval for tokenId, ERC721 owner/vault possession
primary_state_entity: ERC721 ownership or vault possession after transfer
desynchronization_step: approveTransferERC721() sets approval, but transferERC721() does not clear it and there is no revoke path.
strict_match_notes: Permission-management stale approval; excluded from MV-SI recall as SV-SI/access-control-like state inconsistency.

source_root_cause: Failure to clear the approval authorization for NFT resulted in authorized agents being able to transfer the NFT multiple times.
source_exploitation_method: The NFT authorization is not reset.
violated_invariant: A single-token ERC721 transfer approval must be consumed/cleared when the approved transfer executes.
evidence_source: TOSEM permission-management subtype row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 10 H-03; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/10/contracts/contracts/visor/Visor.sol:481-516.
coupled_state_entities: nftApprovals/delegate approval for tokenId, ERC721 owner/vault possession
primary_state_entity: ERC721 ownership or vault possession after transfer
desynchronization_step: transferERC721() transfers the NFT but leaves the token-specific approval set.
strict_match_notes: Single stale approval variable; not MV-SI recall.

source_root_cause: The key manager is not reset.
source_exploitation_method: The attacker repeats the transaction after the key transfer, either returning the key to themselves or transferring it to other recipients.
violated_invariant: After a key transfer to a recipient without a live key, the key manager must be reset to match the new key ownership authority.
evidence_source: TOSEM permission-management subtype row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 54 H-03; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/54/smart-contracts/contracts/mixins/MixinTransfer.sol:109-147 and dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/54/smart-contracts/contracts/mixins/MixinKeys.sol:221-229.
coupled_state_entities: keyManagerOf[tokenId], key ownership/owner record, key expiration state
desynchronization_step: transferFrom() records the recipient as owner when toKey.tokenId == 0 but does not reset keyManagerOf[tokenId] in that branch.
strict_match_notes: Permission stale-state/access-control issue, not MV-SI recall.

source_root_cause: The _setKeyManagerOf function does not clear single-token approvals.
source_exploitation_method: The attacker repeatedly transfers the key back to themselves from the victim without clearing the previous approval after the key transfer.
violated_invariant: Single-token approvals must be cleared whenever transferFrom() moves a key into a new valid ownership/expiration state.
evidence_source: TOSEM permission-management subtype row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 54 H-04; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/54/smart-contracts/contracts/mixins/MixinTransfer.sol:138-147 and dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/54/smart-contracts/contracts/mixins/MixinKeys.sol:221-229,406-412.
primary_state_entity: key ownership and expiration after transferFrom()
desynchronization_step: transferFrom() can call _setKeyManagerOf(tokenId, address(0)) when keyManagerOf is already zero, so _clearApproval() is skipped and approved[tokenId] remains usable.
strict_match_notes: Single stale approval variable; not MV-SI recall.

source_root_cause: Child node states are not considered when unwrapping the parent node.
source_exploitation_method: When unblocking child nodes, the original expiration time management mechanism was disrupted.
violated_invariant: A wrapped parent node expiry must not be set below the expiry of extant child nodes, because child guarantees are bounded by and interpreted relative to the parent.
evidence_source: TOSEM permission-management subtype row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 145 H-02; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/145/contracts/wrapper/NameWrapper.sol:356-365,504-525,825-843,867-879,918-932.
coupled_state_entities: parent node expiry/fuses, child node expiry/fuses, oldExpiry from getData(node), maxExpiry from getData(parentNode)
desynchronization_step: Unwrapping a parent zeroes its oldExpiry despite existing child nodes; rewrapping through setSubnodeOwner then normalizes without the child-expiry constraint.
strict_match_notes: Evaluated repo sheet exists but no same finding tag appears in MV-Bench.

source_root_cause: Reset the user's pendingFlashDecision to default configuration when processing pending governance decisions.
source_exploitation_method: The attacker repeatedly calls withdrawGovernanceAsset to steal funds.

violated_invariant: Each pendingFlashDecision[target][user] withdraw entitlement must correspond to that same user’s locked collateral; burning a rejected decision must invalidate that entitlement rather than resetting it to a withdrawable default config.
evidence_source: TOSEM Incorrect Logic Update row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 78 H-04; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/78/contracts/DAO/FlashGovernanceArbiter.sol:60-79,124-153.
coupled_state_entities: pendingFlashDecision[target][user], flashGovernanceConfig, FlashGovernanceArbiter ERC20 collateral balance
primary_state_entity: locked collateral attributable to a specific target/user decision
external_proxy_state: ERC20 collateral balance held by FlashGovernanceArbiter
desynchronization_step: burnFlashGovernanceAsset() burns/rejects collateral but assigns pendingFlashDecision[target][user] = flashGovernanceConfig instead of deleting the user-specific record.
strict_match_notes: B0 row 78:6 has same repo, same pendingFlashDecision/config state group, same burnFlashGovernanceAsset() desync, and same withdrawGovernanceAsset() transfer sink.

source_root_cause: The _updateRewardsPerToken function early returns when totalSupply is zero.
source_exploitation_method: When minting for the first time, the user mistakenly received all the rewards from the previous reward period.
violated_invariant: rewardsPerToken_.lastUpdated must advance to the current/end time even when totalSupply is zero so future minters cannot claim rewards for an empty-supply interval.
evidence_source: TOSEM Incorrect Logic Update row; Web3Bugs/Code4rena report 25 H-02; local code dataset/Web3Bugs/contracts/25/contracts/utils/token/ERC20Rewards.sol:102-121.
primary_state_entity: reward period clock elapsed while totalSupply == 0
desynchronization_step: _updateRewardsPerToken() returns early on totalSupply == 0 without updating rewardsPerToken_.lastUpdated.
strict_match_notes: Single stale reward checkpoint variable; not MV-SI recall.
